page_title: Tang Zhao Li Hui Guo Rou
text:
Tang Zhao Li Hui Guo Rou (トウチョウレイ＝ホイコーロ, Tōchōrei Hoikōro) is the Third Queen of the Kakin Empire's eight Queens and third wife of Nasubi Hui Guo Rou. She gave him one son, 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei Hui Guo Rou. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 

Appearance

Not much is known about Tang Zhao Li's appearance as she has yet to make a physical appearance in the story. Her portrait is shown in an overview of the princes with their mothers and guards. In this portrait, Tang Zhao Li has short, curly, blonde hair, a thin nose, large eyes, and dark skin. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 374 

Tang Zhao Li appears as a silhouette when the 14 Princes are first introduced. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 However, her hairstyle then does not line up with her later portrait, indicating that her design may not yet have been finalized then.

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Queen Tang Zhao Li's name is first mentioned as mother of 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei when Kakin's 14 Princes are first revealed, and is one of King Nasubi's 8 wives. She is pictured as a silhouette. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 

Shortly after the departure ceremony, an unknown attacker kills five of Woble's guards, including an unnamed guard for Tang Zhao Li. As a result, Kurapika uses his Dowsing Chain on the remaining guards and identifies two guards who are not truly affiliated with Kurapika's employer Oito. The guards are restrained and divulge the Queens' espionage system to Kurapika, Oito, and Woble's other remaining guard to explain themselves before they are murdered by Sayird when he gets manipulated by Momoze's Guardian Spirit Beast. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 

In Hinrigh's investigation to account for the possibility that Hisoka could be a stowaway on the Black Whale. Hinrigh orders his subordinate Connelly to contact Queen Tang Zhao Li and request that she has the army perform a round of passenger I.D. and ticket verifications. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378

Manga Appearances

Translations around the World

Trivia

Every queen of Kakin has a numerical theme on their name that corresponds to their marital status, except for her. Her name doesn't mean "three" in any language, though "chourei" could be a corruption of the English word "three" or the Italian "tre", especially in Southern Italian dialects which pronounce the number "tri"/"tre" with a sound in between a "t" and a "ch".
